The question is asking for the percentage difference between the Republican (r) and Democratic (d) candidates in Connecticut (CT). The "most recent election results" column shows the percentages for each candidate. Since we need to compare the Democratic and Republican candidates' percentages, we should calculate the difference between them. The data shows Chris Murphy (D) with 54.8% and Linda McMahon (R) with 43.1%. We subtract the Republican candidate's percentage from the Democratic candidate's percentage.

The Democratic candidate, Chris Murphy, received 54.8% of the vote, while the Republican candidate, Linda McMahon, received 43.1%. To find the percentage difference between them, subtract the Republican percentage from the Democratic percentage: 54.8% - 43.1% = 11.7%.
